backlog.title: --link Multi-Target Batch Linking
backlog.description: Extend skilldozer's --link flag from single-target (--link <dir>) to multi-target batch (--link <dir> [<dir>...]). The PRD was updated (commit 983672e) to specify batch linking per §8.4, §6.1, §6.3, §6.4, and decisions 21+23, but no implementation code was changed — the current binary exits 2 with "'--link' cannot be combined with tag arguments" when given multiple directories. This phase implements the batch behavior in Go (parser, exclusivity, execution), updates shell completions for multi-position directory completion, and syncs documentation. All other features (skillsdir, discover, resolve, search, check, config, ui, install.sh, --init, --completions, etc.) are fully implemented and unaffected — discovery already follows symlinks (§7.1), so a linked skill resolves by its link name with no new discovery code.

milestones.title: Go Implementation — Parser, Exclusivity, and Batch Execution
milestones.description: Convert the --link code path from single-target to multi-target batch: update the config struct, parseArgs to collect all trailing positionals after --link, exclusivityError to not reject link-collected positionals as tags, runLink to loop over targets with partial-success semantics, the missing-value error message to exact §6.4 wording, the usage text to show <dir> [<dir>...], and all existing + new tests.

tasks.title: Multi-target --link parsing, batch execution, and error semantics
tasks.description: The single atomic change that makes --link work end-to-end for batch operations: config struct field rename, parseArgs multi-positional collection, exclusivityError update, runLink batch loop with per-dir validation and partial success, exact error message wording, usage text, and all test updates (existing tests adapted + new batch tests added).

subtasks.title: Convert --link to multi-target batch: struct, parser, exclusivity, runLink, tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/006_bab1774043df/architecture/codebase_state.md for a complete map of the current single-target --link implementation with exact line numbers. Key facts: (a) config.linkTarget is a `string` at main.go:177; parseArgs captures ONE token after --link (next-token form main.go:403-429, =-form main.go:266-274); exclusivityError rejects trailing positionals as tags at main.go:941-953 (the `if c.link { if hasTags { ... } }` block); runLink processes a single target at main.go:1424-1490; the missing-value guard is at main.go:608-610 with message `skilldozer: --link requires a path to a skill directory`; usage text at main.go:82,98,111 shows `--link <dir>`. (b) There are 19 existing link tests in main_test.go at lines 3491-3984 — see codebase_state.md for the full table of which tests need updating. (c) The critical behavioral change: `TestRunLinkWithTagsExits2` (main_test.go:3576) currently tests `--link /tmp/foo sometag` → exit 2 (tags+mode exclusivity); with batch linking, `sometag` after `--link` is collected as a LINK TARGET (not a tag), so it becomes a validation failure (not a directory) → exit 1. This test MUST be updated. (d) The dashed-follower guard (commit ce01b55) must be preserved: `--link --check` still exits 2 (missing value), NOT consumed as a target.
2. INPUT: The `config` struct at main.go:155-185 (field `linkTarget string` at line 177, `linkMissingValue bool` at line 178). The `parseArgs` function (particularly the `case "--link":` blocks at main.go:266-274 and 403-429, and the default positional handler that appends to `c.tags`). The `exclusivityError` function at main.go:898-973 (particularly the `--link` block at 941-953 that checks `hasTags`). The `runLink` function at main.go:1424-1490. The missing-value guard at main.go:608-610. The `usageText` / `usage()` function (help text at main.go:82, 98, 111). The 19 existing link tests at main_test.go:3491-3984. Helper functions: `expandHome()` (main.go), `skillsdir.Find()`, `skillsdir.HasSkillMD()`.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §8.4 batch linking. Steps: (a) RENAME config struct field `linkTarget string` → `linkTargets []string` (main.go:177). Add a `collectingLink bool` field to track that subsequent positionals should route to linkTargets, not tags. (b) UPDATE parseArgs next-token form (`case "--link":` at main.go:403-429): when --link is seen, set `c.link = true` and `c.collectingLink = true`; if a non-dashed follower exists, append it to `c.linkTargets` (was: assign to `c.linkTarget`); else set `linkMissingValue = true`. PRESERVE the dashed-follower guard: a token starting with `-` after `--link` is NOT consumed as a target — it triggers `linkMissingValue`. (c) UPDATE parseArgs =-form (`case "--link":` at main.go:266-274): set `c.link = true` and `c.collectingLink = true`; if val is non-empty, append to `c.linkTargets`; if empty, set `linkMissingValue`. (d) UPDATE the default positional handler (the `default:` case in parseArgs): if `c.collectingLink` is true, append the token to `c.linkTargets` instead of `c.tags`. This is the KEY change that makes every following positional a link dir. (e) UPDATE exclusivityError (main.go:941-953): the `hasTags` check in the --link block should now only fire for tags BEFORE --link (positionals after --link go to linkTargets, not tags, so c.tags stays empty in normal multi-link usage). The mode-flag exclusivity check (`c.check || c.init || ...`) stays unchanged. REMOVE the `'--link' cannot be combined with tag arguments` error for post-link positionals — they are no longer tags. (f) UPDATE the missing-value guard (main.go:608-610): change the message to exact §6.4 text: `skilldozer: --link requires at least one path to a skill directory`. Also handle the case where `c.link` is true but `len(c.linkTargets) == 0` (e.g., `--link` with only dashed followers consumed) → same exit 2. (g) REWRITE runLink (main.go:1424-1490) to loop over `c.linkTargets` in input order: for each target, absolutize (expandHome + filepath.Abs), validate (existing dir via os.Stat / not store-or-inside via Clean+HasPrefix / HasSkillMD), conflict handling (nothing → os.Symlink; existing symlink → os.Remove + os.Symlink; non-symlink → refuse with stderr message), then on success print linkPath to stdout + `Linked <linkPath> -> <absTarget> (found via <src>)` to stderr, on failure print a stderr line naming the directory. Track `hadErr bool`. After the loop, return 1 if hadErr, else 0. The store resolution (skillsdir.Find) happens ONCE before the loop. (h) UPDATE usageText (main.go:82, 98, 111) to show `--link <dir> [<dir>...]` and describe batch behavior. Add a multi-link example. (i) UPDATE EXISTING TESTS: all tests referencing `c.linkTarget` must use `c.linkTargets` (or `c.linkTargets[0]`): TestParseArgsLinkNextToken (3508), TestParseArgsLinkEquals (3525). Update TestRunLinkWithTagsExits2 (3576): the behavior changes from exit 2 to exit 1 (sometag is now a link target that fails validation). All other existing link tests should pass unchanged (n=1 batch). (j) ADD NEW TESTS: TestRunLinkMultiSuccess (2 dirs → both linked, stdout has both paths in order, exit 0), TestRunLinkMixedBatch (2 valid + 1 invalid → valid linked, invalid on stderr, exit 1, successful links remain), TestRunLinkSingleBadDir (1 invalid → nothing on stdout, exit 1), TestParseArgsLinkCollectsMultiple (--link a b c → linkTargets=[a,b,c], tags empty), TestParseArgsLinkEqualsPlusPositionals (--link=a b → linkTargets=[a,b]), TestRunLinkOrderPreservation (3 dirs → stdout paths in input order).
4. OUTPUT: `skilldozer --link a b c` links all three (exit 0, 3 paths on stdout in order). `skilldozer --link a invalid b` links a, fails on invalid, links b (exit 1, 2 paths on stdout, 1 error on stderr). `skilldozer --link` alone (no dirs) → exit 2, nothing on stdout. `skilldozer --link --check` → exit 2 (dashed follower, missing value). All existing tests pass (adapted). All new tests pass. `go build` succeeds. `go test ./...` passes.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md "Linking skills from elsewhere (`--link`)" section (around line 158) to document batch behavior: multi-link syntax (`skilldozer --link a b c`), partial success (valid links persist if others fail), exit codes (0 all / 1 any fail / 2 no dirs). Update the quick-start example at README.md:~127 to show the batch form. This rides WITH the work — do not create a separate docs subtask.

milestones.title: Shell Completions for Multi-Link Directory Completion
milestones.description: Update the bash, zsh, and fish completion scripts so that after --link appears anywhere in the command line, every subsequent positional position completes to directories (not skill tags). This implements PRD §14.1 rule 5 and the `--link d1 <tab>` behavior matrix row.

tasks.title: Update all three completion scripts for multi-link directory completion
tasks.description: Currently completions only offer directories when the immediately preceding token is --link. After --link d1, the prev arg is d1 (not --link), so tags are offered instead of directories. Update all three scripts to detect --link anywhere in the command line and switch to directory completion at every subsequent position.

subtasks.title: Multi-position directory completion after --link (bash, zsh, fish)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/006_bab1774043df/architecture/codebase_state.md § "Completion Scripts" for the current per-shell --link handling with exact code. Current behavior: (a) bash (completions/skilldozer.bash:41-43) — only completes dirs via `compgen -d` when `$prev` is exactly `--link`; after `--link d1`, prev=d1, falls through to tag completion. (b) zsh (completions/_skilldozer:49-51) — `_arguments` treats `--link` as single-value; subsequent positionals get tags. (c) fish (completions/skilldozer.fish:37-39) — `-r` flag makes only the NEXT arg a file arg; after that, falls through to tag completion. The lockstep invariant (§14.4) requires all three files to stay in sync with the flag set in main.go parseArgs(). The TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk test in main_test.go verifies that embedded (go:embed) bytes match on-disk files byte-for-byte — after editing completions/*, a REBUILD (`go build`) is required for `--completions` to reflect changes.
2. INPUT: completions/skilldozer.bash (79 lines), completions/_skilldozer (66 lines), completions/skilldozer.fish (69 lines). The `--link` handling per shell is documented in codebase_state.md. The flag list is frozen to main.go parseArgs() per §14.4.
3. LOGIC: Update all three scripts so that after `--link` appears ANYWHERE in the command line before the current cursor position, EVERY subsequent positional completes to DIRECTORIES (not skill tags). PRD §14.1 rule 5: "when `--link` is present, every following positional completes to directories (it links many, §8.4)." §14.2: "`--link <dir> [<dir>...]` — one or more directory values → offer file/dir completion at every position after `--link`." Per-shell approach: (a) BASH: before the existing `$prev` case dispatch, scan `${COMP_WORDS[@]}` for `--link`; if found at any index < $COMP_CWORD, set `COMPREPLY=($(compgen -d -- "$cur"))` and `return 0` (bypassing tag/flag completion entirely). This is a simple prefix check. (b) ZSH: the current `_arguments` spec treats `--link` as single-value (`':directory:_files'`). Change it so post-`--link` positionals also get `_files`. Approach: split into a two-phase `_arguments` call or use a custom dispatcher that checks `words` for `--link`. A clean approach: add `'*::link-dirs:_files'` as a catch-all after `--link` — but _arguments may need restructuring. Alternatively, add a guard at the top of the function: if `--link` is in `$words`, call `_files` directly and return. (c) FISH: fish completions are declarative. Add a condition that checks if `--link` has been seen using `__fish_seen_subcommand_from` or a custom function. Approach: add `complete -c skilldozer -n 'string match -q -- "--link" (commandline -opc)' -a '(__fish_complete_directories)' -d "Skill directory to link"` — this fires at every position after `--link` appears. KEEP all existing behavior intact: skills-first on bare `<tab>` (no --link present), long-form-only flags, --init/--store dir completion, --search nothing, --shell enum, §14.7 list-ambiguous options. The ONLY change is the multi-position directory behavior after --link.
4. OUTPUT: `skilldozer --link <tab>` → directories (unchanged). `skilldozer --link d1 <tab>` → directories (NEW). `skilldozer --link d1 d2 <tab>` → directories (NEW). `skilldozer <tab>` → skills (unchanged). `skilldozer -<tab>` → long-form flags (unchanged). After rebuild, `skilldozer --completions --shell bash` emits the updated script. TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk still passes.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change beyond the completion behavior itself, which is already documented in README.md §8 and PRD §14. The `--link d1 <tab>` case is the natural extension of the documented `--link <tab>` case. If README §8 mentions the specific completions for --link, add a note that every position after --link completes dirs.

milestones.title: Documentation Sync & Acceptance Verification
milestones.description: Final Mode B documentation sweep: verify the full §13 acceptance suite passes (including the new multi-link and mixed-batch test cases), sweep README.md for any remaining single-target --link wording in overview/cross-cutting sections, and ensure the changeset is coherent end-to-end.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Cross-cutting documentation sweep that only makes sense once the whole changeset is in place. Verifies §13 acceptance, updates README overview sections that reference --link, and ensures no stale single-target wording remains in cross-cutting mentions (mode-flags list, completions section, quick-start).

subtasks.title: Sweep README overview + verify §13 acceptance
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md has multiple --link mentions beyond the main section (which was updated by P1.M1.T1.S1 as Mode A): mode-flags discussion at ~line 152 (`The flags that take a value — --store, --search, --shell, and --link — all exit 2 when given as the last token`), completions section at ~line 322 (flag list) and ~line 326 (`skilldozer --init <tab>, skilldozer --link <tab>, and skilldozer --store <tab>`). The §13 acceptance criteria (PRD §13) include explicit multi-link and mixed-batch test cases that must all pass.
2. INPUT: README.md at /home/dustin/projects/skilldozer/README.md (updated by P1.M1.T1.S1). The built binary (from P1.M1.T1.S1). The §13 acceptance script block from PRD §13. The updated completion scripts (from P1.M2.T1.S1).
3. LOGIC: (a) RUN the full §13 acceptance suite from PRD §13 against the built binary — every line must pass, including the multi-link (`echo "multi-link OK"`), mixed-batch (`echo "multi-link-partial OK"`), single-bad-dir (`echo "link-non-skill-refused OK"`), and missing-value (`echo "link-missing-value OK"`) cases. If any fail, document the failure and identify the root cause. (b) SWEEP README.md for remaining single-target --link wording in cross-cutting/overview sections: the mode-flags discussion (~line 152) should mention that --link collects multiple positionals; the completions section (~line 326) should note `skilldozer --link d1 <tab>` completes directories; the quick-start example should show the batch form. (c) VERIFY no stale text claims --link takes a single directory or uses tag-exclusivity language for post-link positionals. (d) CHECK constraints/guardrails section (README §9 / PRD §15 §9 / PRD §17) — ensure --link is described consistently with batch behavior.
4. OUTPUT: README.md fully coherent with batch --link behavior across all sections. All §13 acceptance tests pass (document the run output). No stale single-target --link wording remains anywhere in the repo.
5. DOCS: This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task (Mode B per §5).
